title: Schoenidium laterale
taxonomy: Schoenidium laterale is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Orchidoideae. It belongs to the genus Schoenidium. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic analysis. The exact classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Angiospermophyta, Class: Liliopsida, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Subfamily: Orchidoideae, Genus: Schoenidium, Species: S. laterale. Further research may be needed to confirm the accuracy of this classification.

intro: Schoenidium laterale, also known as Uvaria lateralis, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Annonaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Schoenidium laterale include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, with the most common preparations being used for wound healing, skin care, and fever reduction.
